meaning: adj.不能反驳的,不能驳倒的
examples: The pictures provide irrefutable evidence of the incident.
It is irrefutable that poverty and hunger are two of the most critical consequences of overcrowding.
These ironclad details are irrefutable proof in black and white.
The need to modernize our transmission lines and to expand their capacity is irrefutable.
Inevitably, this step will be perceived as irrefutable evidence that East-West military confrontation can never return.
